page: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 11250 GB NVMe SSD configuration, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 28 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and database operations. The server achieved 404.61 GB/sec for large-block read-only memory bandwidth and 201.28 GB/sec for large-block write operations, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 24079.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 511.7 GB/sec. Database operations scored 47385.11 on Passmark, and Redis SET operations reached 98.01 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU scores falling in the middle 50%.

Given its high-density local NVMe storage and strong memory bandwidth, the i8g.12xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for data-intensive workloads. The arm64 architecture provides qualitative cost-efficiency benefits by delivering high multi-core throughput and memory performance per unit of power and resource. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, and memory-intensive data processing tasks. It is less optimal for workloads requiring specialized x86 instructions or high-speed single-threaded execution.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of memory and a bundled 11250 GB NVMe SSD, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block reads reaching 404.61 GB/sec and writes at 201.28 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core performance including a Geekbench multi-core score of 24079.0. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 47385.11 and 98.01 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance is average, the server's high storage density and memory throughput make it highly suitable for large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.

tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized server featuring dedicated cores, high-capacity local NVMe SSDs, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.

Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier memory bandwidth of 404.61 GB/sec read and 201.28 GB/sec write.

Best suited for large-scale databases, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
